pattern: Greedily anchoring each group at the smallest remaining value forces the only legal run of k consecutive numbers, so a frequency count plus ordered consumption decides feasibility.
hints: If the array length is not a multiple of k, the split is impossible before you even look at the values.
The smallest value still present must begin a group, and that group is forced to be exactly that value through value plus k minus one.
Count occurrences, then repeatedly take the smallest available number as a group start and decrement the next k consecutive counts; if any required number is missing, it cannot be done.

edge_inputs.description: many small groups with a gap
right length but values cannot form consecutive runs
whole array must be one group of length k but a value is missing

stress.description: constructed splittable arrays so feasibility holds and large counts stress the consumption loop
stress.note: built as a union of consecutive runs so the true answer is typically true; correctness still verified by oracle agreement
